findings.message: New `redis-exporter` Compose service exposes Redis metrics on :9121 but nothing scrapes it: infra/observability/prometheus/prometheus.yml has no `redis` scrape job (only app, prometheus, otel-collector, postgres). The exporter runs in every environment via this overlay, so this is a prod runtime surface (Redis) with zero observability — Prometheus never collects the metrics, making the exporter dead weight. Contrast postgres-exporter, which has a matching scrape job, alert rules, and dashboard.
findings.suggestion:
Add a scrape job to prometheus.yml, e.g.
  - job_name: redis
    static_configs:
      - targets: ["redis-exporter:9121"]

findings.message: The added redis-exporter has no corresponding Prometheus alert rule. Every other monitored surface ships an alerts file (postgres_alerts.yml, otel_collector_alerts.yml, prometheus_alerts.yml, slo_alerts.yml) under infra/observability/prometheus/alerts/, but there is no redis_alerts.yml. Redis availability/memory issues would go unalerted.
findings.suggestion: Add infra/observability/prometheus/alerts/redis_alerts.yml with rules on redis_up / redis_memory_used_bytes to match the coverage of the other exporters.

findings.message: The added redis-exporter has no Grafana dashboard. infra/observability/grafana/dashboards/ contains postgres.json, otel-collector.json, prometheus.json, and slo.json but no redis.json, so the newly collected Redis metrics would not be visualized for the same surface its peers cover.
findings.suggestion: Add infra/observability/grafana/dashboards/redis.json so the new Redis surface has a panel, consistent with postgres/otel-collector/prometheus.
